message: fix: parse full markdown sections for close comments

@@ -90,14 +90,15 @@ Current main already implements the requested suppression option for noisy exec
 
 What I checked:
 
-- **Runtime suppression for node exec lifecycle events:** The node event handler explicitly checks `cfg.tools?.exec?.notifyOnExit !== false` and returns early before enqueueing system events when the option is disabled.
+- **Runtime suppression for node exec lifecycle events:** The node event handler explicitly checks `cfg.tools?.exec?.notifyOnExit !== false` and returns early before enqueueing system events when the option is disabled. ([`src/gateway/server-node-events.ts:632`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/gateway/server-node-events.ts#L632), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Regression coverage for started/finished/denied:** Tests verify that `exec.started`, `exec.finished`, and `exec.denied` do not enqueue system events or wake heartbeat when `tools.exec.notifyOnExit` is false. ([`src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts:341`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/gateway/server-node-events.test.ts#L341), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Config schema includes the option:** The runtime config schema accepts `tools.exec.notifyOnExit` as a boolean option. ([`src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts:469`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts#L469), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **User-facing help matches the requested behavior:** Schema help says `tools.exec.notifyOnExit` controls both backgrounded exec exit notifications and node exec lifecycle events. ([`src/config/schema.help.ts:581`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/config/schema.help.ts#L581), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
 
 So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
 
 Review notes: reviewed against [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2); fix evidence: commit [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2).
 
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Fix evidence: commit [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2).

@@ -94,14 +94,16 @@ Current OpenClaw already provides gateway-handled, no-model account switching an
 
 What I checked:
 
-- **Gateway handles slash commands/directives before the model:** Docs state commands are handled by the Gateway, directives are stripped before the model sees them, and inline shortcuts run immediately before normal model flow.
+- **Gateway handles slash commands/directives before the model:** Docs state commands are handled by the Gateway, directives are stripped before the model sees them, and inline shortcuts run immediately before normal model flow. ([`docs/tools/slash-commands.md:9`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/docs/tools/slash-commands.md#L9), [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+- **Fast path bypasses queue and model:** Docs explicitly say command-only messages from allowlisted senders are handled immediately with a bypass of queue and model. ([`docs/tools/slash-commands.md:197`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/docs/tools/slash-commands.md#L197), [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+- **Per-session auth-profile switching already exists:** FAQ documents `/model opus@anthropic:default` and `/model opus@anthropic:work`, and says `/model status` shows which auth profile is active/next. ([`docs/help/faq.md:2329`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/docs/help/faq.md#L2329), [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+- **Model/profile switch is persisted and acknowledged without agent output:** Directive handling persists the requested model/auth-profile override and immediately returns a reply like `Model set ... Auth profile set ...`. ([`src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives-apply.ts:251`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5/src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-directives-apply.ts#L251), [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5))
+- **Global auth-order edits and restart are already exposed as gateway commands:** Docs expose `/config show|get|set|unset` for `openclaw.json` and `/restart`; the handlers write validated config to disk and trigger gateway restart. `auth.order[provider]` is the explicit rotation order knob. ([`src/auto-reply/reply/commands-config.ts:169`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/auto-reply/reply/commands-config.ts#L169), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
 
 So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
 
 Review notes: reviewed against [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2); fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).

@@ -102,14 +102,17 @@ Current main already provides the requested capability in practice: durable JSON
 
 What I checked:
 
-- **Session persistence design:** The session-management deep dive says OpenClaw persists sessions in two layers and that `<sessionId>.jsonl` is an append-only transcript storing the actual conversation, tool calls, and compaction summaries.
+- **Session persistence design:** The session-management deep dive says OpenClaw persists sessions in two layers and that `<sessionId>.jsonl` is an append-only transcript storing the actual conversation, tool calls, and compaction summaries. ([`docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md:42`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md#L42), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Transcript format is JSONL:** The same doc defines transcript structure as JSONL with a session header followed by message/custom/compaction entries. ([`docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md:165`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/reference/session-management-compaction.md#L165), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Transcript writer persists session files:** The transcript writer creates the session JSONL header on disk, including the session id and timestamp, as the durable transcript file. ([`src/config/sessions/transcript.ts:18`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/config/sessions/transcript.ts#L18), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Transcript persistence is verified in tests:** The transcript test reads the written JSONL file and asserts the next line is a `message` entry containing the assistant text. ([`src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts:75`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts#L75), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Compaction does not delete history by default:** `agents.defaults.compaction.truncateAfterCompaction` only rewrites the session JSONL when explicitly enabled, and the documented default is `false`. ([`src/config/schema.help.ts:1224`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/config/schema.help.ts#L1224), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Pre-compaction snapshots exist:** Compaction checkpoint capture copies the live transcript to a sibling `.checkpoint.*.jsonl` snapshot before compaction. ([`src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.ts:72`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.ts#L72), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
 
 So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
 
 Review notes: reviewed against [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2); fix evidence: release [v2026.4.22](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/releases/tag/v2026.4.22), commit [00bd2cf7a376](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/00bd2cf7a376f1fba26291c6c4766f1f15cbdfa5).
 
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). @@ -95,14 +95,16 @@ Current `main` now implements post-compaction refresh injection: OpenClaw expose
 
 What I checked:
 
-- **Config surface exists:** `AgentCompactionConfig` now includes `postCompactionSections`, documented as AGENTS.md H2/H3 sections injected after compaction, defaulting to `Session Startup` and `Red Lines`.
+- **Config surface exists:** `AgentCompactionConfig` now includes `postCompactionSections`, documented as AGENTS.md H2/H3 sections injected after compaction, defaulting to `Session Startup` and `Red Lines`. ([`src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts:451`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc/src/config/types.agent-defaults.ts#L451), [1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc))
+- **Docs describe the feature:** The gateway config reference documents `agents.defaults.compaction.postCompactionSections` as the post-compaction reinjection mechanism and shows it in the config example. ([`docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md:1416`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc/docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md#L1416), [1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc))
+- **Refresh prompt builder:** `readPostCompactionContext()` builds a `[Post-compaction context refresh]` prompt that explicitly says the session was compacted and instructs the agent to rerun startup / read required files before responding. ([`src/auto-reply/reply/post-compaction-context.ts:149`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc/src/auto-reply/reply/post-compaction-context.ts#L149), [1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc))
+- **Injected before the next run:** `runPreflightCompactionIfNeeded()` now calls `appendPostCompactionRefreshPrompt()` immediately after successful compaction, attaching the refresh text to `followupRun.run.extraSystemPrompt` before the next assistant turn. ([`src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts:510`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc/src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-memory.ts#L510), [1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc))
+- **Regression coverage:** A follow-up runner test asserts that after preflight compaction the next embedded run receives `Post-compaction context refresh` plus the AGENTS startup instruction in `extraSystemPrompt`. ([`src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts:858`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc/src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts#L858), [1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc))
 
 So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.
 
 Review notes: reviewed against [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2); fix evidence: commit [1daa552d5f42](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/1daa552d5f429f9c57daa3ed050c34239b8a22dc).

@@ -93,14 +93,17 @@ The request is a valid capability, but current main’s documented scope and ext
 
 What I checked:
 
-- **Vision scope is plugin-first:** OpenClaw’s vision says core stays lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high.
+- **Vision scope is plugin-first:** OpenClaw’s [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says core stays lean, optional capability should usually ship as plugins, and the bar for adding optional plugins to core is intentionally high. ([`VISION.md:55`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/VISION.md#L55), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Hook-only plugins are an intended pattern:** The plugin architecture explicitly supports hook-only plugins and says prompt mutation work should prefer before_prompt_build. ([`docs/plugins/architecture.md:83`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/plugins/architecture.md#L83), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Per-plugin config already exists:** The configuration reference documents plugin-scoped config objects and prompt-injection hook policy under plugins.entries.<id>, which is the intended place for optional scanning configuration. ([`docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md:2916`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md#L2916), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Core already exposes the needed interception points:** The agent loop documents hooks for before_prompt_build, before_tool_call, tool_result_persist, and inbound/outbound message handling, which are the relevant seams for scanning untrusted inputs and tool outputs. ([`docs/concepts/agent-loop.md:94`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/concepts/agent-loop.md#L94), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Tool-result middleware already lives in plugin space:** Tokenjuice is documented as an optional plugin that transforms tool_result content after execution, showing that this class of prompt/tool-output middleware is already modeled as a plugin concern. ([`docs/tools/tokenjuice.md:10`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/tools/tokenjuice.md#L10), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **No native top-level promptInjection config on main:** A repo search found prompt-injection config only in plugin hook policy (`plugins.entries.*.hooks.allowPromptInjection`), not as a core `security.promptInjection` schema key. ([cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
 
 So I’m closing this as a scope-fit item for the plugin/community path rather than keeping it open as an OpenClaw core request.
 
 Review notes: reviewed against [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2).
 
-Codex evidence: Reviewed against main [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2). Specific fixed release/commit was not determined by this review.

@@ -86,14 +86,17 @@ Issue #7740 requests bundling an externally maintained skill, but current OpenCl
 
 What I checked:
 
-- **Vision: new skills go to ClawHub first:** Current project vision says bundled skills remain baseline UX only; new skills should be published to ClawHub first, and core skill additions should be rare.
+- **Vision: new skills go to ClawHub first:** Current [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) says bundled skills remain baseline UX only; new skills should be published to ClawHub first, and core skill additions should be rare. ([`VISION.md:68`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/VISION.md#L68), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Vision: not merging new core skills:** [`VISION.md`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/main/VISION.md) guardrails explicitly list 'New core skills when they can live on ClawHub' under items the project will not merge for now. ([`VISION.md:101`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/VISION.md#L101), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Plugin docs: community/external distribution is first-class:** User-facing plugin docs describe external community plugins and a ClawHub-first install resolver instead of bundling optional capabilities into core. ([`docs/tools/plugin.md:11`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/tools/plugin.md#L11), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Skills docs: ClawHub is the public skill registry:** Current skills docs direct users to ClawHub for skill discovery/install/update, and to the separate `clawhub` CLI for publish/sync workflows. ([`docs/tools/skills.md:109`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/tools/skills.md#L109), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))

+- **ClawHub guide: official registry for skills and plugins:** The ClawHub docs state that ClawHub is the public registry for OpenClaw skills and plugins, with native OpenClaw install/search/update flows and separate publish/sync tooling. ([`docs/tools/clawhub.md:10`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/tools/clawhub.md#L10), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))

 What I checked:
 
-- **Retry behavior is documented for model providers and transient channel errors:** Docs say model providers use normal short SDK retries, long retry-after waits are surfaced so model failover can rotate to another auth profile or fallback model, and Telegram retries transient timeout/connect/reset/unavailable errors.
+- **Retry behavior is documented for model providers and transient channel errors:** Docs say model providers use normal short SDK retries, long retry-after waits are surfaced so model failover can rotate to another auth profile or fallback model, and Telegram retries transient timeout/connect/reset/unavailable errors. ([`docs/concepts/retry.md:28`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/docs/concepts/retry.md#L28), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Long retry-after responses are converted into immediate failover signals:** The guarded model fetch detects retryable 408/409/429/5xx responses over the configured cap and rewrites them with `x-should-retry: false`, so control returns to OpenClaw’s fallback logic instead of stalling. ([`src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts:58`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/agents/provider-transport-fetch.ts#L58), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Agent turns are wrapped in model fallback handling:** `agent-command` runs each turn through `runWithModelFallback(...)`, passing the provider/model overrides into the actual attempt runner and treating later attempts as fallback retries. ([`src/agents/agent-command.ts:870`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/agents/agent-command.ts#L870), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Connection/API failures are classified as retryable timeout/failover reasons:** Current tests assert that `ETIMEDOUT`, `ECONNREFUSED`, `ECONNRESET`, `EAI_AGAIN`, `Connection error.`, `fetch failed`, and similar network failures are treated as `timeout`, which is the failover/retry lane. ([`src/agents/failover-error.test.ts:512`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/agents/failover-error.test.ts#L512), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Fallback succeeds after timeout-class failures:** `runWithModelFallback` is covered by a test where an `ETIMEDOUT` failure on the first model retries via fallback and succeeds on the next candidate. ([`src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts:1271`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/agents/model-fallback.test.ts#L1271), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
+- **Embedded-run fallback covers service-unavailable failures without persisting a hard cooldown:** Current end-to-end fallback coverage treats `LLM error: service unavailable` as a timeout-lane failure and successfully switches to a fallback provider. ([`src/agents/model-fallback.run-embedded.e2e.test.ts:599`](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/blob/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2/src/agents/model-fallback.run-embedded.e2e.test.ts#L599), [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2))
 
 So I’m closing this as already implemented rather than keeping a duplicate issue open.

 Review notes: reviewed against [cec34821754a](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/cec34821754ad935c780805f3116f99449d46ac2).
 
-
-Current main already exposes the raw machine-readable inputs this proposal wants to ingest: gateway file logs are JSONL (`docs/logging.md:14`), session transcripts live at `~/.openclaw/agents/<agentId>/sessions/<sessionId>.jsonl` (`docs/concepts/session.md:79`), and cache trace already writes JSONL (`docs/gateway/configuration-reference.md:3592`). OpenClaw’s observability/export model is also already plugin-scoped: diagnostics exporters only attach when the exporter plugin is enabled, and the documented first-party export path is the `diagnostics-otel` plugin (`docs/logging.md:180`, `docs/logging.md:258`).
-
-Given the repo vision that core stays lean and optional capability should usually ship as plugins (`VISION.md:55`), a JSONL -> SQLite analytics pipeline is a better fit as a ClawHub/community plugin or standalone package than new OpenClaw core infrastructure. The existing comment pointing to ClawMetry also shows this approach already works against current log/session surfaces. If someone wants to pursue this, the right venue is the community plugin/tool path rather than core.
